Name: BrewDog Wingman
Summary: Animated marketing campaign for BrewDog's newest session IPA
Overview:
The Eagle has landed 🦅🍻

Introducing our latest video campaign for BrewDog. WingMan Session IPA is BrewDog's latest release, and they wanted it to launch with that classic BrewDog flare. The campaign has been an unforgettable opportunity to get together and collaborate with some pretty talented people.
Our Design Thinking:
BrewDog wanted this beer to have style, so they worked with Earthling Studio to develop a character and brand identity for Wingman.  After the initial designs were signed off, the character and brand elements were handed to our team, who developed a storyboard and concept for the release campaign. With a tight turnaround approaching, we worked with Illustrator Tom Warne to sketch the scenes and then returned them to our Motion Design team to add the final touches.


Since the initial launch we've continued to make social media content to promote Wingman.
Finished Product:
The project was an epic challenge; with tight deadlines, our team worked smart to craft a compelling narrative sequence without relying on complex animation techniques. The end result is pretty kick-ass, and we're thrilled that BrewDog can depend on us to get the job done.


Motion Design: Ryan Knight

Brand Identity: Earthling Studio

Illustration: Tom Warne

Project Management: Flo Slater
Alt text - Main image: Character animation of punk rock bird revealing BrewDog's latest beer.

Name: Collective Continuum
Summary: Immersive web experience for revolutionary venture capitialism
Overview:
Collective Continuum is the group working to change the world, one impact venture at a time. Their collective offers a unique approach in that philanthropy is not an afterthought; it's an integral part of their ethos. Collective Continuum actively seeks to support modern ideas with the foundations of traditional Islamic values.  

They are rethinking the impact venture works, and so they contacted us to craft a website experience that sets their mission apart. The client came to us with the motif of space exploration as the launch point for visual experimentation.
Our Design Thinking:
This project was a refreshing challenge for our team to create something immersive and imaginative while staying rooted in the story behind Collective Continuum.

We started by exploring three potential design routes. Our explorations were driven by compelling storytelling and tested to see what resonated with their demographic. We approached this brief in a storyboard format, adding in scroll interactions to transport the user through their screen into a web experience like no other.

The designs were just part of the effort; we needed to implement them into a fully responsive live website. The project was developed on Webflow with the backbone of a Lottie animation made in After Effects.

Our unique combination of design, Webflow, and technical knowledge allows us to get things done and overcome obstacles that wouldn't be possible otherwise.
Finished Product:
This project was a learning experience that helped us refine our process for creating complex, interactive websites. Our designers worked collaboratively across our UI/UX, animation, and development specialties to craft a unique web experience. A big thank you to the team at Collective Continuum for trusting us with their site.

We can't wait to do more immersive sites in 2024!

Check out the live site to experience it for yourself: https://www.collectivecontinuum.com/

Storyboard + Design | Natalia Triandofilidi
Animation + Design Support | Samuel Higginson
Project Management| Emma James and Danielle Willetts
Development | Radiance + Austin Joseph

Name: Oddsjam
Overview:
OddsJam is an innovative solution designed by Stanford engineers to identify mathematically profitable betting opportunities. It uses the fastest real-time data to help users beat the books. With significant earnings on offer, OddsJam required an interface that was as smart as the science behind it to attract new subscribers.

‍We've worked with OddsJam for years on the design and testing of their product. We strive to keep OddsJam up to date and constantly improve by evolving the consistent component library and refining clear and direct user flows.
Our Design Thinking:
We updated the UI to give it a sleek, contemporary appearance that increased excitement and made it simple to identify the best bets to place. This was achieved by switching the platform's default colour system to dark mode. By opting for dark mode, we prioritised user comfort, aesthetic appeal, and thematic alignment, ultimately enriching the overall user experience of the platform.

Simplicity was a key factor as this app uses high levels of complex information. We needed to make sure the user journey through the app was easy to use and navigate through. Our approach incorporated principles of usability and accessibility to deliver a seamless and engaging experience.
Finished Product: The introduction of dark mode was a popular choice among users, increasing user engagement and brand consistency. The intuitive design elements and seamless navigation pathways allowed smoother interactions, resulting in higher engagement with the platform.

Name: Polaron
Overview: When Polaron approached us, we took the opportunity to shape a digital experience that reflects the groundbreaking nature of their AI technology. Our challenge was to create a brand that captures their innovative spirit while ensuring the site remains user-friendly for their technical audience.
Our Design Thinking:
With a rich palette of deep blues and crisp whites, we aimed for a sophisticated yet approachable feel. The layout is clean and intuitive, guiding users seamlessly through the site.

Motion graphics were essential to our vision, showcasing Polaron's capability to transform 2D images into dynamic 3D models. These animations breathe life into the website, turning complex technology into a captivating experience.

We chose fonts that project professionalism while remaining accessible, balancing clarity and modernity. The overall design reflects Polaron's energetic approach, inviting visitors to dive deeper into their offerings and grasp the power of their AI tools.
Finished Product: The redesigned site not only captivates visually but also performs exceptionally. Polaron's platform communicates its innovations with a striking brand identity with clarity and impact. User feedback has been overwhelmingly positive, with visitors engaging more deeply and sparking meaningful conversations about their technology.

Name: Qrone
Summary: Motion design for the future of networking
Overview: Qrone came to us with a vision: to transform the traditional business card into a dynamic digital touchpoint. Our mission? Position their digital business card platform as a high-end, future-forward solution that appeals to modern professionals, preserving the elegance of traditional networking.
Our Design Thinking:
Our creative strategy embraced the platform’s sophistication, blending high-end 3D renders with polished 2D animation. Rich, dark colour palettes and dramatic lighting underscored Qrone’s premium appeal, while seamless transitions reflected its effortless user experience.

Each scene highlighted key interactions—from NFC card taps to instant profile sharing—illustrating how Qrone elevates networking by removing traditional barriers and providing a comprehensive digital solution.

‍
Finished Product: The result was a striking video that positions Qrone at the crossroads of premium technology and accessible networking. We crafted a narrative that bridges classic networking values with digital innovation, establishing Qrone as an aspirational brand for today’s professionals.

Name: RegTrail
Summary: Modern product design for news & insights platform
Overview:
The market is in unprecedented times regarding regulation changes; it can take time for businesses of all sizes to keep up. Manually scanning different news platforms takes time and resources.

RegTrail fills a necessary gap in the market - providing curated regulatory news & insights for the energy and commodity community. The web platform aims to deliver the most important news to your email to save you time and offer convenience. They keep you up to date and ensure you get the complete picture of regulator updates from hundreds of sources.

The founder of RegTrail reached out to us to develop a platform that aesthetically and functionally met their mission of creating an accessible and intuitive platform. Our primary goal was to craft a direct user route and experience.
Our Design Thinking: The client turned to our expertise in establishing effective communication with the target customer. Therefore, our objective was to research the user path's best possibilities. First, we worked out the user flow and architecture of the product's experience. Then we developed a visual style and palette reflecting a clean, clear, forward-thinking product. After deciding on the core visual elements, geometric solid shapes featuring a subtle blue gradient and glass morphic icons, we expanded these elements into a fully-fledged design system and applied them to the low-fidelity screens.
Finished Product:
The end product empowers the user by delivering custom-tailored insights to their inbox.

We guided the client in selecting the most crucial scenarios throughout the user journey design stage and concentrated on these components in the design. The platform is user-friendly, intelligible, and visually appealing. Without sacrificing the integrity of the user path, we added several intriguing graphical solutions. The client claims that many consumers wanted to embrace the platform's new format right away during the design phase.

Check out a live demo of the product today: https://www.regtrail.com/
